sourceMovie.title: Blade Runner 2049
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Blade Runner 2049 for the same reason it broke through in 2017: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Denis Villeneuve keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The science fiction and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Blade Runner
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Blade Runner (1982) belongs to the same conversation as Blade Runner 2049 — both anchor their science-fiction in drama emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: The Terminator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Blade Runner 2049's science-fiction stayed with you, The Terminator (1984) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The action through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Insurgent
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Blade Runner 2049 excels in science-fiction, Insurgent (2015) doubles down on action to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Alita: Battle Angel
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Alita: Battle Angel (2019) is the natural next step after Blade Runner 2049: same science-fiction DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and action elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: The Lobster
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Blade Runner 2049 who respond to its drama will find The Lobster (2015) immediately familiar. Both films share a comedy sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: The Matrix Resurrections
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Blade Runner 2049 fans keep landing on The Matrix Resurrections (2021) — the science-fiction is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Terminator: Dark Fate
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Terminator: Dark Fate draws from the same science-fiction well as Blade Runner 2049, channeling action in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2019 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Upgrade
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Upgrade (2018) captures the science-fiction appeal of Blade Runner 2049 without simply imitating it. The action streak running through both films is what puts Upgrade at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Changeling
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The drama appeal of Blade Runner 2049 — that tense edge, the crime — translates cleanly into Changeling (2008). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: The Chronicles of Riddick
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind The Chronicles of Riddick (2004) clearly understood what makes science-fiction work — the same quality that keeps Blade Runner 2049 rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

faq.question: What should I watch after Blade Runner 2049?
faq.answer: Try Blade Runner, The Terminator, Insurgent—each shares DNA with Blade Runner 2049 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Blade Runner 2049?
faq.answer: Blade Runner 2049 (2017) is best described as Science Fiction, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Blade Runner 2049 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Blade Runner Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
